hero.title: System Before Tactics
hero.subtitle: We don't just build websites — we help align the system that your marketing, sales, delivery, and retention live inside. Your digital presence becomes a purposeful commercial tool, not another overhead cost.

tiers.name: Phase I: Diagnostic & Stabilization
tiers.subtitle: Foundations & Clarity
tiers.description: Every engagement starts with a Commercial EKG — a stage-by-stage audit that identifies where friction, weak handoffs, and missing proof are interrupting the journey. We establish clear positioning, improve routing, and create the first layer of visibility.
includes: Clear positioning across profiles and owned properties
Gateway routing for low-friction buyer entry

tiers.name: Phase II: Orchestration
tiers.subtitle: Visibility & Execution — Recommended
tiers.description: Move from scattered activity to a Managed Nervous System — the first reliable layer of visibility and automation. We tighten stage definitions, improve handoffs, and create structured follow-through using the tools your business already has.
includes: Information Fusion for automated signal capture
Website or microsite restructuring around the canonical journey
Recurring operating reviews tied to buyer and customer signals

tiers.name: Phase III: Transition
tiers.subtitle: Governance & The Alabrida Bridge
tiers.description: Strengthen governance, rationalize your tool stack, and prepare the handoff to deeper Revenue Architecture or owned infrastructure work. This phase makes the system durable enough to outlast any single engagement.
includes: Data model hardening and system documentation
Transition planning for deeper architecture work
The Alabrida Bridge — readiness for owned infrastructure

timeline.title: The Path to a Unified Commercial Engine

phases.name: The Commercial EKG
phases.description: Diagnose the current system and identify the biggest sources of revenue friction.
milestones: Review each of the five stages from the buyer's point of view
Identify weak handoffs, missing proof, and structural friction
Place the business in the maturity tier that best matches the whole system
Clarify stage definitions, buyer signals, and ownership
Create a staged plan based on actual system conditions

phases.name: Managed Nervous System
phases.description: Install the first layer of visibility and automation using the current stack.
milestones: Create a shared source of truth across teams
Install Information Fusion for automated signal capture
Improve handoffs between marketing, sales, delivery, and retention
Structure follow-up that continues conversations without pressure
Build operating reviews tied to real buyer and customer signals

phases.name: Unified Commercial Engine
phases.description: Align the full commercial journey around shared rules and cleaner handoffs.
milestones: Strengthen governance with shared definitions and stage rules
Rationalize tools so the stack serves the operating model
Harden the data model and document the system
Prepare transition planning for deeper architecture if needed
Move toward a system that improves without relying on heroics

curriculum.description: Running the stage-by-stage audit to identify where friction, weak handoffs, and missing proof are interrupting the journey.
Creating clear positioning across profiles, search, and owned properties so the right buyers can find you.
Building low-friction entry points that route buyer interest to a meaningful next step.
Restructuring the website to support buyer evaluation with proof, framing, and examples.
Installing the first layer of visibility and automation to improve follow-through and reduce manual work.
Connecting offline networking to digital follow-up — signatures, cards, and trackable entry points.
Capturing useful buyer signals automatically so the business spends less time reconstructing what happened.
Building intent-specific pages that help buyers evaluate fit without unnecessary noise.
Establishing a repeatable content and review rhythm to maintain visibility across the perimeter.
Deploying relevant proof and trust signals that reduce decision risk for the buyer.
Auditing the current stack to make sure tools serve the operating model, not the other way around.
Preparing the transition from Guide-led orchestration to deeper Revenue Architecture when the business is ready.
